_meta.purpose: Comprehensive handoff briefing for ChatGPT to verify repo structure, validate progress, and stress-test refresh chain v3.0 after Peter pushes to GitHub. This covers everything built or updated since the last collective sync.

executive_summary: Major consolidation push completed 2026-04-17. All CoDaWork 2026 materials consolidated into self-contained codawork2026/ folder (65 files). AI refresh chain updated to v3.0 with archive-on-update protocol. Two new general conference handouts built. Geochemistry extension (40,666 real rocks) and Gold Standard Working Example (10-step chain) added to evidence base. CoDaWork talk deck built (16 slides). Geometric mean empirical proof articulated as the headline finding.

refresh_chain_v3.description: All 4 AI refresh files updated with complete current state. Previous versions archived.
key_additions_to_fast_refresh: Restructured file_map into refresh_chain / canonical_sources / briefings / codawork2026_package
refresh_chain_v3.refresh_chain_concept: The refresh chain is now a self-sustaining knowledge management protocol. Each pass compresses sessions into canonical signal and archives old state. History grows in archive/; the live chain stays lean and current. This is compositional knowledge management — EITT applied to the project itself.

codawork2026_consolidation.description: Self-contained top-level folder with everything for Coimbra. 78 aged drafts archived to archive/pre-codawork2026/.
subfolders: presentation/ — Talk PPTX (16 slides) + submitted abstract
experiments/ — Python builders, Working Example (11 plots + JSON)
extended/ — Extended results, EITT handout, coffee table, 2 general conference handouts
science/ — Cheatsheet, adversarial tests, formulas, safety boundaries, WHY_IT_WORKS, HESSIAN_BOUND
codawork2026_consolidation.nothing_outside_needed: True. This folder can be handed to anyone at Coimbra as a complete, self-verifying package.

geochemistry_extension.data: Ball 2022 Global Igneous Geochemistry Dataset + AGDB3 (Alaska)
geochemistry_extension.anomaly: Foidite (sole failure — deep mantle phase chaos, not a method failure)
geochemistry_extension.significance: First real-data geochemistry validation. Brings total experiment chain to 6 independent domains.

gold_standard_working_example.description: Complete 10-step Higgins Decomposition chain with pedagogical commentary from 5 analytical viewpoints. Includes fabricated counterexample to show the contrast at each step.

talk_deck.design: NAVY/ICE_BLUE/TEAL/GOLD palette, dark slides for title/close, light for content
talk_deck.strategy: Stanley Steamer — their tools, their geometry, our application, their language

conference_handouts.description: Two general-audience handouts replacing personalized primers. Designed for walk-up distribution at Coimbra.

handout_A.audience: CoDa practitioners who already work with time series
handout_A.approach: Protocol → results → EITT proof → geometric mean evidence → open question → reading path

handout_B.audience: Static/geochemistry practitioners who need the bridge to temporal analysis
handout_B.approach: Familiar ground (40,666 rocks, TAS, oxides) → bridge table (static vs temporal) → energy results → EITT → experiment chain → reading path

conference_handouts.both_direct_to: GitHub learning path at codawork2026/ for self-study after conference

geometric_mean_empirical_proof.headline: EITT gives measurable proof that geometric mean is the correct aggregator for compositional time series
geometric_mean_empirical_proof.evidence: Geometric mean preserves Shannon entropy at 0.18-1.02% variation. Arithmetic mean destroys 20-40% of entropy.
geometric_mean_empirical_proof.significance: This is not theory — it is measurement. The CoDa community knows geometric mean is correct from Aitchison geometry. EITT now provides empirical, measurable evidence anyone can reproduce.

experiment_chain_complete.description: Six independent experiments, all LEGITIMATE, spanning 5 domains.
experiment_chain_complete.total_EITT_proofs: 6 (energy Proof 1 daily, Proof 2 monthly, chemistry 500k, geochemistry 40k real rocks, Bell Test, NGFS scenarios)

canonical_values_to_verify.note: ChatGPT should verify these against HUF_FAST_REFRESH.json v3.0
canonical_values_to_verify.eitt_proof_1: 0.18% entropy variation, 341:1 compression, daily→annual European electricity
canonical_values_to_verify.eitt_proof_2: Mean 1.02%, all 6 countries below 2%, monthly→annual EMBER
canonical_values_to_verify.geochemistry: 40,666 rocks, 8 oxides, 37/39 TAS pass, Foidite sole anomaly
canonical_values_to_verify.eitt_full_name: Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer (NEVER Ternary, NEVER Temporal)

verification_tasks_for_chatgpt.instruction: Read ai-refresh/HUF_FAST_REFRESH.json v3.0 cold. Can you orient on the project in one read? Are the canonical values self-consistent? Any drift from the numbers listed above?
Read ai-refresh/HUF_INTEGRITY_MANIFEST.json v3.0. Verify the hash chain concept makes sense. Check that the known drift patterns are comprehensive enough to catch common AI hallucination modes.
Browse codawork2026/. Is the package self-contained? Can someone at Coimbra use it without touching anything outside this folder? Any missing pieces?
Review the geometric mean empirical proof claim. Is '0.18% vs 20-40%' a fair characterization of the evidence? Are there caveats we should add?

The refresh chain is now: FAST_REFRESH → INTEGRITY_MANIFEST → ADMIN → PHASE_MARKERS → INDEX.json. Each pass tightens. Does this sequence make sense? Can a new AI start at FAST_REFRESH and become productively oriented? Any circular dependencies or contradictions?

Read the two new handouts in codawork2026/extended/ (CoDa_TimeSeries and Geochemistry_Bridge). Are they pitched at the right level for their audiences? Do they accurately represent the evidence? Do they correctly direct to the GitHub learning path?

outstanding_work.abstract_v4_amendment: Drafted 2026-04-14, pending send to codawork2026@coda-association.org before 2026-05-01
outstanding_work.lit_verification_001: Five-AI literature verification in progress. Grok branch complete. Four branches outstanding. Consolidated verdict expected 2026-04-24.
outstanding_work.phase_2_active: EITT hardening for broader adoption. See PHASE_MARKERS.json P02 for full roadmap.
outstanding_work.poster_pdf: Not yet built (lower priority — talk deck takes precedence)

how_to_use_this_briefing: Peter will push to GitHub, then paste or reference this briefing in a ChatGPT session. ChatGPT should read FAST_REFRESH.json first for full context, then work through the verification tasks above. Report any drift, contradictions, or missing pieces back to Peter.
